issue.message: A newer version of com.android.application than 8.9.0 is available: 9.2.1. (There is also a newer version of 8.9.𝑥 available, if upgrading to 9.2.1 is difficult: 8.9.3)
issue.explanation: This detector looks for usage of the Android Gradle Plugin where the version you are using is not the current stable release. Using older versions is fine, and there are cases where you deliberately want to stick with an older version. However, you may simply not be aware that a more recent version is available, and that is what this lint check helps find.

issue.message: A newer version of com.android.application than 8.9.0 is available: 9.2.1. (There is also a newer version of 8.9.𝑥 available, if upgrading to 9.2.1 is difficult: 8.9.3)

issue.message: `checkClientTrusted` is empty, which could cause insecure network traffic due to trusting arbitrary TLS/SSL certificates presented by peers
issue.explanation: This check looks for X509TrustManager implementations whose `checkServerTrusted` or `checkClientTrusted` methods do nothing (thus trusting any certificate chain) which could result in insecure network traffic caused by trusting arbitrary TLS/SSL certificates presented by peers.
issue.errorLine1: override fun checkClientTrusted(chain: Array<X509Certificate>, authType: String) {}

issue.message: `checkServerTrusted` is empty, which could cause insecure network traffic due to trusting arbitrary TLS/SSL certificates presented by peers
issue.explanation: This check looks for X509TrustManager implementations whose `checkServerTrusted` or `checkClientTrusted` methods do nothing (thus trusting any certificate chain) which could result in insecure network traffic caused by trusting arbitrary TLS/SSL certificates presented by peers.
issue.errorLine1: override fun checkServerTrusted(chain: Array<X509Certificate>, authType: String) {}

issue.message: Implementing a custom `X509TrustManager` is error-prone and likely to be insecure. It is likely to disable certificate validation altogether, and is non-trivial to implement correctly without calling Android's default implementation.
issue.explanation: This check looks for custom `X509TrustManager` implementations.

issue.message: Insecure Base Configuration
issue.explanation: Permitting cleartext traffic could allow eavesdroppers to intercept data sent by your app, which impacts the privacy of your users. Consider only allowing encrypted traffic by setting the `cleartextTrafficPermitted` tag to `false`.

issue.message: The attribute `android:allowBackup` is deprecated from Android 12 and higher and may be removed in future versions. Consider adding the attribute `android:dataExtractionRules` specifying an `@xml` resource which configures cloud backups and device transfers on Android 12 and higher.
issue.explanation:
Before Android 12, the attributes `android:allowBackup` and `android:fullBackupContent` were used to configure all forms of backup, including cloud backups, device-to-device transfers and adb backup.
In Android 12 and higher, these attributes have been deprecated and will only apply to cloud backups. You should instead use the attribute `android:dataExtractionRules`, specifying an `@xml` resource that configures which files to back up, for cloud backups and for device-to-device transfers, separately. If your `minSdkVersion` supports older versions, you'll still want to specify an `android:fullBackupContent` resource if the default behavior is not right for your app.

issue.message: Unnecessary; `SDK_INT` is always >= 30
issue.explanation:
This check flags version checks that are not necessary, because the `minSdkVersion` (or surrounding known API level) is already at least as high as the version checked for.
Similarly, it also looks for resources in `-vNN` folders, such as `values-v14` where the version qualifier is less than or equal to the `minSdkVersion`, where the contents should be merged into the best folder.

issue.message: This folder configuration (`v26`) is unnecessary; `minSdkVersion` is 30. Merge all the resources in this folder into `mipmap-anydpi`.
issue.explanation:
This check flags version checks that are not necessary, because the `minSdkVersion` (or surrounding known API level) is already at least as high as the version checked for.
Similarly, it also looks for resources in `-vNN` folders, such as `values-v14` where the version qualifier is less than or equal to the `minSdkVersion`, where the contents should be merged into the best folder.
